lessons.title: The very first steps
lessons.status: Template preview — awaiting Alan’s real completion
lessons.lede: The experiment begins here: Father William Most’s course, a notebook, a microphone and a determination to discover whether the Church’s language can become something genuinely usable.
lessons.aim: Meet the first Latin patterns and begin thinking beyond translation
lessons.dayNote: Day 1. Excited. A little daunted. This template will be replaced by Alan’s genuine account after he completes the lesson. — A.W.G.
lessons.curiosityText: The first step is to read through Lesson I in Father William Most’s Latin by the Natural Method.
lessons.curiositySample: Read first. Notice second. Explain only after the text has had a chance to speak.

studySteps.title: Write out the vocabulary
studySteps.text: Write each word in Latin and English. Keep the two languages together so the vocabulary can be checked and recalled.

studySteps.title: Copy both texts by hand
studySteps.text: Copy the lesson texts from the book to become accustomed to forming and seeing the Latin words.

studySteps.title: Think in plain English
studySteps.text: Record what the endings appear to be doing before reaching for a technical explanation.

studySteps.title: Complete Father Most’s exercise
studySteps.text: Work from the original book. This site records the experience without reproducing the textbook exercise.

checklist: Read Lesson I in Father Most’s textbook
Make a genuine first reading before studying
Write the vocabulary in Latin and English
Copy the lesson texts by hand
Record what seemed easy, difficult or surprising
Return for a second reading after study
lessons.todayLatin: This is a provisional template. After Alan completes Lesson I, this space will record what was easy, what was difficult, what remained challenging and what should happen next.
lessons.notebookCaption: Alan’s Lesson I notebook photograph will appear here after the lesson is completed.
lessons.churchPlaceholder: The Lesson I connection is loaded from the separate Church-connections record.

lessons.title: Nouns: first patterns
lessons.lede: Lesson II is open as a working example of the repeatable publishing system. Its wording and evidence will be replaced after Alan has genuinely completed the lesson.
lessons.aim: Test how a second lesson flows through the same reusable page structure
lessons.dayNote: Lesson II template preview. No learning claim is being made yet. This note will be replaced by Alan’s real bus-journey reflection. — A.W.G.
lessons.curiosityTitle: What changes when a noun changes its job?
lessons.curiosityText: This is a demonstration prompt rather than Alan’s finished explanation. The real curiosity hook will be written from what he actually notices in Lesson II.
lessons.curiositySample: Same template. New lesson. Real evidence replaces every placeholder.

studySteps.title: Read the original lesson
studySteps.text: Begin with Father Most’s book and make an unpolished first attempt.

studySteps.title: Write the vocabulary
studySteps.text: Copy the new words in Latin and English, preserving any uncertainty for later checking.

studySteps.title: Copy and notice
studySteps.text: Write the lesson material by hand and note repeated noun forms or endings without inventing conclusions.

studySteps.title: Record the real reaction
studySteps.text: Replace this demonstration text with Alan’s observations, corrections and questions after study.

checklist: Read Lesson II in Father Most’s textbook
Make a first reading before studying
Copy the relevant material by hand
Send the journal thoughts for publication
lessons.todayLatin: This demonstration proves that Lesson II can use the same page without duplicating the site design. When Alan completes it, the placeholder will be replaced by his real easy, difficult, challenging and next-step reflection.
lessons.notebookCaption: Test position for Alan’s Lesson II notebook photograph.
lessons.churchPlaceholder: A proportionate Church connection will be added only after the lesson has been studied and checked.
